headline: Heat advisory peaks today — harvest beans before storms.
weather_notes: Work before 8 AM today — Heat Advisory is active through tonight with 100°F closing every afternoon outdoor window.
Skip zone irrigation today and tomorrow — watered yesterday; hold to see if Tuesday's 50% storm chance delivers first meaningful rain.
Harvest beans and blackberries this morning — 100°F softens pods and damages ripe fruit on the cane by afternoon.
Hold all foliar sprays and feeds until Thursday — 50–70% storm chances Tuesday through Wednesday will wash off same-day applications.
Check e1–e4 transplants after Tuesday's storms — 30-day plants risk stem lodging from heavy monsoon downpours.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 10 days overdue; Tuesday's 50% and Wednesday's 71% storm chances make early morning today or Tuesday the last clear window before monsoon weather hammers the canes.

suggested_tasks.title: Check c2–c4 beans for harvest-ready pods
suggested_tasks.description: At 53 days, Jade Bush (c3/c4) and Red Swan (c2) are at their 55-day window — pick today before Tuesday's 50% storm chance knocks mature pods.

suggested_tasks.title: Pick blackberries before noon
suggested_tasks.description: Triple Crown berries observed June 14 are at peak ripeness — harvest this morning before 100°F heat softens ripe fruit on the cane.
